segments.text: Good day, ladies and gentlemen.
segments.translation: 女士们，先生们，大家好。

segments.text: I have been asked today to talk to you about the urban landscape.
segments.translation: 今天的演讲是关于城市景观。

segments.text: There are two major areas that I will focus on in my talk: how vegetation can have a significant effect on urban climate, and how we can better plan our cities using trees to provide a more comfortable environment for us to live in.
segments.translation: 我的演讲主要针对两点：植被是如何对城市气候产生重大影响的。如何利用树木更好地规划更加舒适的城市居住环境。

segments.text: Trees can have a significant impact on our cities.
segments.translation: 树木对我们城市的影响是巨大的。

segments.text: They can make a city, as a whole, a bit less windy or a bit more windy, if that's what you want.
segments.translation: 他们能使城市成为一个整体，少些风或者需要的话，多些风。

segments.text: They can make it a bit cooler if it's a hot summer day in an Australian city, or they can make it a bit more humid if it's a dry inland city.
segments.translation: 如果是澳洲市区炎热的夏天，树木能使天气凉快一点，又或者如果是干燥的内陆城市，树木能使空气湿润些。

segments.text: On the local scale - that is, in particular areas within the city - trees can make the local area more shady, cooler, more humid and much less windy.
segments.translation: 在本地区域，尤其是城市内某些区域，树木能使该区域更多树荫，更凉快，更湿润，也同时大大减少风力。

segments.text: In fact trees and planting of various kinds can be used to make city streets actually less dangerous in particular areas.
segments.translation: 事实上，在市内特定的区域，树木和各类植被能使城市路面危险度降低一些。

segments.text: How do trees do all that, you ask?
segments.translation: 树木是如何做到这些的呢？

segments.text: Well, the main difference between a tree and a building is a tree has got an internal mechanism to keep the temperature regulated.
segments.translation: 嗯，树木和建筑最主要的区别是树木自身的内部机制能调节温度。

segments.text: It evaporates water through its leaves and that means that the temperature of the leaves is never very far from our own body temperature.
segments.translation: 水分通过树叶蒸发，这意味着树叶的温度不会和人类的体温相差太远。

segments.text: The temperature of a building surface on a hot sunny day can easily be twenty degrees more than our temperature.
segments.translation: 艳阳炙烤下的建筑物表面温度轻易就能超过体温20度。

segments.text: Trees, on the other hand, remain cooler than buildings because they sweat.
segments.translation: 而另一方面，树木能持续保持比建筑物温度低，因为他们能出汗。

segments.text: This means that they can humidify the air and cool it - a property which can be exploited to improve the local climate.
segments.translation: 这就意味这他们能湿润空气，然后降温，而这个特质能被用于改善本地气候。

segments.text: Trees can also help break the force of winds.
segments.translation: 树木还能帮助分解风力。

segments.text: The reason that high buildings make it windier at ground level is that, as the wind goes higher and higher, it goes faster and faster.
segments.translation: 之所以高楼的底层风力更大是因为随着风向增高，风速会越来越快。

segments.text: When the wind hits the building, it has to go somewhere.
segments.translation: 当风撞击到建筑物时，风必须吹向一个方向。

segments.text: Some of it goes over the top and some goes around the sides of the building, forcing those high level winds down to ground level.
segments.translation: 有些风走向建筑物顶端，而有些则围绕建筑物，这迫使高处的风走向建筑物底层。

segments.text: That doesn't happen when you have trees.
segments.translation: 当有树木的时候，这就不会发生。

segments.text: Trees filter the wind and considerably reduce it, preventing those very large strong gusts that you so often find around tall buildings.
segments.translation: 树木能过滤风并很大程度上减低风力，阻止形成高楼周围常见的强风。

segments.text: Another problem in built-up areas is that traffic noise is intensified by tall buildings.
segments.translation: 在高楼林立区域的另外一个问题是高楼会加强交通噪音。

segments.text: By planting a belt of trees at the side of the road, you can make things a little quieter, but much of the vehicle noise still goes through the trees.
segments.translation: 通过在路两旁种植树带能使马路安静些，但是大部分的噪音还是会穿树而过。

segments.text: Trees can also help reduce the amount of noise in the surroundings, although the effect is not as large as people like to think.
segments.translation: 树木还能减低周围环境的噪音，虽然不如我们想象中有效。

segments.text: Low- frequency noise, in particular, just goes through the trees as though they aren't there.
segments.translation: 特别是低频噪音无视树木的存在，直接穿过他们释放出来。

segments.text: Although trees can significantly improve the local climate, they do however take up a lot of space.
segments.translation: 虽说树木能大大改善本地气候，但确实也很占空间。

segments.text: There are root systems to consider and branches blocking windows and so on.
segments.translation: 例如根系，树枝挡着窗等。

segments.text: It may therefore be difficult to fit trees into the local landscape.
segments.translation: 所以将树放置在本地景观中可能有困难。

segments.text: There is not a great deal you can do if you have what we call a street canyon - a whole set of high-rises enclosed in a narrow street.
segments.translation: 如果碰到街道峡谷，就是高楼成排街道狭窄，那也就无能为力了。

segments.text: Trees need water to grow.
segments.translation: 树木生长需要水。

segments.text: They also need some sunlight to grow and you need room to put them.
segments.translation: 他们也需要一些阳光来成长，你需要空间来种植。

segments.text: If you have the chance of knocking buildings down and replacing them, then suddenly you can start looking at different ways to design the streets and to introduce...
segments.translation: 如果有机会推倒建筑再建，那时就可以开始用不同的方式审视街道设计了，引入......
